questions.q: Who were Vidura's parents?
options: The sage Vyasa and a palace serving maid
The sage Vyasa and Queen Ambika
The sage Parashara and Satyavati
King Shantanu and a fisherwoman
questions.explanation: Vyasa fathered Vidura on the serving maid whom Queen Ambika sent to him in her own place.

questions.q: Vidura is described in the epic as the incarnation of which deity?
options: Indra, king of the gods
Dharma, the god of righteousness
Agni, the god of fire
Vayu, the god of wind
questions.explanation: Vidura was Dharma himself, born among mortals because of the sage Mandavya's curse.

questions.q: Why was Vidura considered ineligible for the throne of Hastinapura?
options: He was born blind like his elder brother
He renounced kingship with a lifelong vow
He was born to a serving maid of low rank
He was cursed by a sage to never wear a crown
questions.explanation: Because his mother was a shudra serving maid, Vidura was barred from kingship despite his wisdom.

questions.q: What position did Vidura hold at the Kuru court?
options: Commander of the royal armies
Royal priest and astrologer
Keeper of the royal treasury
Chief counselor and minister
questions.explanation: Vidura served as the chief counselor of Hastinapura, advising Dhritarashtra throughout his reign.

questions.q: Which two kings were Vidura's half-brothers?
options: Bhishma and Vichitravirya
Dhritarashtra and Pandu
Duryodhana and Yudhishthira
Chitrangada and Shantanu
questions.explanation: Vyasa fathered Dhritarashtra on Ambika, Pandu on Ambalika, and Vidura on Ambika's maid, making the three half-brothers.

questions.q: Vidura's secret warning saved the Pandavas from which plot?
options: The burning of the house of lac at Varanavata
The poisoning of Bhima's food by Duryodhana
An ambush on the road to Draupadi's swayamvara
A trap laid inside the Chakravyuha formation
questions.explanation: Vidura's coded warning alerted Yudhishthira to Duryodhana's plan to burn the Pandavas in the lac palace.

questions.q: By what name is Vidura's celebrated discourse on wise conduct and statecraft known?
options: The Anu Gita
The Sanatsujatiya
The Vidura Niti
The Yaksha Prashna
questions.explanation: The Vidura Niti, his counsel to Dhritarashtra in the Udyoga Parva, remains a classic manual of ethics and statecraft.

questions.q: Which event did Vidura strongly urge Dhritarashtra to forbid?
options: The tournament of the Kuru princes
Karna's coronation as king of Anga
Yudhishthira's Rajasuya sacrifice
The game of dice against the Pandavas
questions.explanation: Vidura warned that the dice game would destroy the family, but Dhritarashtra let it proceed for Duryodhana's sake.

questions.q: During his peace embassy to Hastinapura, in whose home did Krishna choose to stay and eat?
options: Vidura's modest house
Duryodhana's royal palace
Bhishma's stately quarters
Drona's teaching grounds
questions.explanation: Krishna refused Duryodhana's lavish hospitality and dined instead at the humble home of the devoted Vidura.

questions.q: For which quality was Vidura most renowned throughout the epic?
options: Unmatched skill with the bow
Wisdom and truthful counsel
Mastery over the game of dice
Strength surpassing all wrestlers
questions.explanation: Vidura was famed as the wisest man at the Kuru court, who always spoke the truth regardless of consequence.

questions.q: The god Dharma was born as Vidura because of a curse from which sage?
options: Durvasa
Kindama
Mandavya
Parashara
questions.explanation: The sage Mandavya cursed Dharma to be born of a shudra womb for punishing him unjustly, and Dharma was born as Vidura.

questions.q: Why did Vyasa father a child with a serving maid rather than with Queen Ambika a second time?
options: Satyavati commanded that the maid bear an heir
Ambika had already borne a healthy royal son
Vyasa himself chose the maid for her great virtue
Ambika, dreading Vyasa's appearance, sent her maid in her place
questions.explanation: Terrified of facing Vyasa again, Ambika dressed her maid in her own ornaments and sent her instead, and the maid's respectful welcome earned a wise, flawless son.

questions.q: Apart from the spoken warning, how did Vidura help the Pandavas survive at Varanavata?
options: He sent a skilled miner to dig an escape tunnel
He bribed Purochana to abandon the plot
He had soldiers secretly surround the lac palace
He persuaded Duryodhana to recall the architect
questions.explanation: Vidura dispatched a trusted miner who dug the underground tunnel through which the Pandavas escaped the burning palace.

questions.q: In what form did Vidura deliver his warning about the lac house to Yudhishthira?
options: As a written message hidden inside a gift
As riddling speech in a dialect only Yudhishthira understood
Through a dream sent with the help of the god Dharma
Through a song performed by a wandering court minstrel
questions.explanation: Vidura spoke in veiled riddles in a tongue the others could not follow, hinting at fire and escape, and Yudhishthira alone grasped the meaning.

questions.q: What counsel did Vidura give when Duryodhana was born amid terrible omens?
options: Send the child to be raised far away in Gandhara
Dedicate the child to the service of a temple
Abandon the one child to save the family and the line
Crown the child immediately to appease the angry gods
questions.explanation: Reading the dreadful omens, Vidura and the brahmins urged Dhritarashtra to cast away the child for the family's sake, but the king refused.

questions.q: On what occasion did Vidura deliver the long discourse called the Vidura Niti?
options: At Yudhishthira's coronation ceremony in Indraprastha
Beside Bhishma's arrow bed in the days after the great war
At the wedding of the Pandavas and Draupadi in Panchala
During a sleepless night when an anxious Dhritarashtra summoned him
questions.explanation: Unable to sleep while awaiting Sanjaya's report from the Pandavas, Dhritarashtra called Vidura, who counselled him through the night.

questions.q: While Draupadi was being humiliated in the dice hall, what did Vidura do?
options: He denounced the game and demanded the assembly answer her question
He quietly left the hall in protest and departed for the forest
He challenged Shakuni to a counter-game to win back her freedom
He sent a swift messenger to summon Krishna to the court
questions.explanation: Vidura alone condemned the proceedings openly, insisting that the assembly answer Draupadi's question on dharma.

questions.q: In whose house did Kunti stay while her sons spent thirteen years in exile?
options: In Bhishma's quarters within the palace
In the house of Vidura
In her kinsmen's palace in the Yadava lands
In Drupada's palace at Kampilya
questions.explanation: Kunti remained behind in Hastinapura and lived under Vidura's protection throughout the Pandavas' exile.

questions.q: Years after the war, Vidura left Hastinapura for the forest in the company of which group?
options: Krishna, Balarama, and the Yadava elders
Yuyutsu, Kripa, and the surviving Kaurava widows
Dhritarashtra, Gandhari, and Kunti
Yudhishthira, Draupadi, and the four Pandavas
questions.explanation: When Dhritarashtra finally retired to the forest with Gandhari and Kunti, Vidura accompanied them into retreat.

questions.q: How did Vidura's life come to an end?
options: He died on the bed of arrows beside Bhishma
He perished in the forest fire with Dhritarashtra
He drowned in the Ganga during a final pilgrimage
Wasted by austerities, he merged his life-force into Yudhishthira
questions.explanation: When Yudhishthira found the emaciated Vidura in the forest, the sage fixed his gaze on the king and passed his life-force into him by yogic power.

questions.q: Why was Yudhishthira told not to cremate Vidura's body?
options: A divine voice said he had died as a renunciate in yoga and needed no rites
Gandhari demanded the body be carried back to Hastinapura
Vyasa wished to revive him through a sage's special boon
The body had already been claimed by the river goddess Ganga
questions.explanation: An unseen celestial voice declared that Vidura had died as a yati absorbed in yoga and that his body should not be burned.

questions.q: Vidura taught that dharma protects those who protect it, and does what to those who destroy it?
options: Forgives them after a hundred births
Destroys them in turn
Abandons them to the mercy of kings
Tests them with ever greater suffering
questions.explanation: The maxim that dharma destroys its destroyers and protects its protectors is traditionally cited from Vidura's counsel, and appears likewise in the dharmashastras.

questions.q: How had the sage Mandavya, whose curse caused Vidura's birth, been wrongly punished?
options: He was exiled for sheltering a fugitive prince
He was blinded for watching a forbidden royal ritual
He was impaled on a stake when robbers hid stolen goods in his hermitage
He was drowned for refusing to bless a childless king's heir
questions.explanation: Mistaken for an accomplice when thieves stashed loot in his hermitage, the silent sage was impaled, earning the name Animandavya.

questions.q: Why did Mandavya curse the god Dharma to be born as a mortal?
options: Dharma had refused to appear when summoned for testimony
Dharma had favored the robbers who framed the sage
Dharma had denied the sage his rightful entry into heaven
Dharma had punished him cruelly for a trivial sin of childhood
questions.explanation: Learning he had been impaled for piercing insects as a small boy, Mandavya ruled that childhood acts are no sin and cursed Dharma to a mortal birth in a shudra womb.

questions.q: In the epic, Vidura is repeatedly addressed as Kshatta. What did the epithet signify?
options: His birth from a high-born father and a serving woman
His office as the chief royal charioteer
His command over the palace guards
His direct descent from King Kuru himself
questions.explanation: Kshatta denotes one born of a mixed union, marking Vidura's birth from Vyasa and a shudra maid.

questions.q: Whom did Vidura marry?
options: A princess of Gandhara, kinswoman of Shakuni
A daughter of King Devaka born to a shudra wife
A daughter of the chief minister of Panchala
A forest hermit's daughter devoted to austerity
questions.explanation: Bhishma arranged Vidura's marriage to King Devaka's daughter by a shudra wife, a match suited to his mixed birth.

questions.q: When Dhritarashtra once dismissed Vidura in anger for siding with the Pandavas, where did Vidura go?
options: To Dwarka, to seek refuge with Krishna
To Gandhara, to confront Shakuni's kinsmen
To the Kamyaka forest, to join the exiled Pandavas
To the Himalayas, to undertake final austerities
questions.explanation: Vidura went to the Pandavas in the Kamyaka forest, and the remorseful king soon sent Sanjaya to bring him back.

questions.q: Why could Vidura's life-force pass into Yudhishthira at the moment of his death?
options: Yudhishthira held a boon allowing him to absorb the souls of kin
Vyasa had bound their two fates with a single mantra at birth
Both had drunk from the same enchanted lake of the Yaksha
Both were embodiments of the same god, Dharma
questions.explanation: Vidura was Dharma incarnate and Yudhishthira was Dharma's son, so the one being merged naturally into the other.

questions.q: Before Krishna's peace embassy reached the court, what did Vidura warn him about?
options: That Duryodhana planned treachery and might try to seize the envoy
That Bhishma would refuse to admit him into the assembly
That Karna had sworn to challenge him at the city gates
That Dhritarashtra would offer him half the kingdom as a bribe
questions.explanation: Vidura cautioned Krishna that the mission was futile and that the wicked Duryodhana might even attempt to seize him, a design Duryodhana indeed harbored.

questions.q: After Draupadi's swayamvara, Vidura brought Dhritarashtra news that first delighted and then dismayed the king. What was it?
options: Duryodhana had won Draupadi but refused to marry her
Draupadi had been won, and the victors were the Pandavas, alive and well
Karna had won Draupadi for the house of the Kauravas
Drupada had cancelled the contest and declared war on the Kurus
questions.explanation: Hearing that Draupadi had chosen, Dhritarashtra assumed Duryodhana had won her, until Vidura revealed that the Pandavas had survived the lac house and triumphed.
